outbreaks.name: MV Hondius Cruise Ship Hantavirus Outbreak
outbreaks.description: Multi-country hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel. Primary case likely acquired infection in Argentina during bird-watching trip pre-cruise.
outbreaks.transmissionType: Primary environmental exposure (pre-cruise in Argentina); Limited person-to-person transmission reported

vesselDetails.name: MV Hondius
vesselDetails.route: South Atlantic cruise including Antarctica, South Georgia, Tristan da Cunha, Saint Helena, Ascension Island

outbreaks.trend: Stabilizing - no new cases reported after May 8
outbreaks.riskAssessment: WHO assesses global risk as LOW; US risk EXTREMELY LOW; no US cases from this outbreak

outbreaks.name: Argentina - Hantavirus Cases Surge (Climate-Related)
outbreaks.description: Argentina experiencing sharp increase in hantavirus cases due to milder winters allowing larger rodent populations in Patagonia and central Argentina.
outbreaks.location: Patagonia, Central Argentina (Buenos Aires region, Provinces)
outbreaks.transmissionType: Environmental - rodent droppings (primary); Climate change impact on rodent populations

environmentalFactors.description: Milder, shorter winters in Patagonia and central Argentina have allowed larger populations of long-tailed pygmy rice rats (Andes virus carriers) to survive and reproduce more frequently
environmentalFactors.climateImpact: Warming temperatures = extended rodent breeding season
environmentalFactors.yearOverYearChange: Cases have almost doubled compared to 2025

outbreaks.epidemiologicalContext: Highest number of infections since 2018; Significant increase from previous year
outbreaks.trend: Increasing - seasonal autumn increase expected to continue

outbreaks.name: Chile - Hantavirus Cases (Spring Season)
outbreaks.description: Chile experiencing endemic hantavirus activity during spring season with confirmed cases across multiple regions.
outbreaks.seasonalPattern: Spring season outbreak (September-May in Southern Hemisphere)

outbreaks.name: USA Southwest - Hantavirus Endemic Activity
outbreaks.description: Ongoing endemic hantavirus activity in southwestern United States. No cases linked to cruise ship outbreak among 17 US passengers.
outbreaks.location: Southwest USA (Arizona, New Mexico, Colorado, Utah, Nevada, California)
outbreaks.virusType: Sin Nombre Virus (SNV) - different from Andes
outbreaks.transmissionType: Environmental - rodent droppings (deer mice primary vector)
outbreaks.cruiseShipPassengerStatus: 17 US passengers aboard MV Hondius - all tested negative

mostSevereOutbreak.name: MV Hondius Cruise Ship
mostSevereOutbreak.reason: Highest CFR due to small sample size and severe Andes virus cases

secondMostSevere.name: Argentina
secondMostSevere.reason: Ongoing surge with high mortality rate

globalStatistics.emergingTrend: Argentina and Chile showing seasonal increase (autumn in Southern Hemisphere) due to climate change allowing larger rodent populations
globalStatistics.globalRiskAssessment: LOW - WHO assessment as of May 9, 2026
globalStatistics.USSpecificRisk: EXTREMELY LOW - No cruise-related cases in US
globalStatistics.pandemiaPotentialNote: Kalshi prediction market shows only 21% chance of global outbreak concern in 2026

newestOutbreakReports.headline: Argentina lethal hantavirus cases rising - Blame climate change
newestOutbreakReports.key_finding: Argentina hantavirus cases have almost doubled; warming temperatures allow larger rodent populations

newestOutbreakReports.headline: WHO Director-General will travel to Tenerife for disembarkment
newestOutbreakReports.key_finding: MV Hondius repatriation in progress; passenger distribution to 23 countries managed

newestOutbreakReports.headline: Chile, Argentina report rise in deadly hantavirus cases
newestOutbreakReports.key_finding: Both South American countries experiencing seasonal increase with high fatality rates

metadata.reportAccuracy: Based on official government and WHO sources as of May 9, 2026
disclaimers: Numbers may change as investigations continue
MV Hondius cases may increase as passengers show symptoms during incubation period
Climate-driven rodent population changes in Argentina/Chile expected to maintain seasonal increase through June
